code.text: COPD, GOLD group with prior exacerbation

note.text: 40 pack-year former smoker. Chronic productive cough and progressive exertional dyspnea over several years.

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale contents of 1 capsule once daily via HandiHaler device

medicationCodeableConcept.text: Albuterol HFA 90 mcg - rescue inhaler, PRN

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4-6 hours as needed for dyspnea

note.text: Quit smoking January 2016 after 34 years of use.

coding.display: Cigarettes smoked total (pack per year) - Reported
Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Eosinophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

resource.conclusion: Post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC 56% confirms persistent airflow obstruction, GOLD stage 2 (moderate). FEV1 51% of predicted.
Hyperinflated lungs with flattened hemidiaphragms and increased retrosternal airspace, consistent with COPD. Mild bibasilar bullous changes. No acute infiltrate or effusion.

coding.display: CBC panel - Blood by Automated count

resource.conclusion: Mild secondary erythrocytosis (Hgb 17.2, Hct 51%) consistent with chronic hypoxemia. Eosinophils low-normal (non-eosinophilic COPD phenotype); ICS unlikely to provide additional benefit.

text.div: Patient reports chronic productive cough and dyspnea on moderate exertion (mMRC grade 2), stable over past 6 months. Former smoker, 40 pack-years, quit January 2016. No recent hospitalizations.

VS: BP 138/84, HR 88, RR 20, SpO2 93% RA, Temp 36.7C. Distant breath sounds, mild expiratory wheeze bilaterally. Spirometry: FEV1 1.8L (51% predicted), FVC 3.2L, FEV1/FVC 56% (GOLD 2, moderate). CXR: hyperinflation, flattened diaphragms, no acute process. Hgb 17.2 (secondary erythrocytosis), eosinophils low-normal.
COPD, GOLD stage 2 (moderate), non-eosinophilic phenotype, clinically stable.

Continue tiotropium (Spiriva) daily and albuterol PRN. Pneumococcal and annual influenza vaccination reviewed. Pulmonary rehabilitation referral discussed. Follow-up in 12 months or sooner if exacerbation.

coding.display: Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive airways disease
Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive airways disease

dosageInstruction.text: Take 4 tablets (40 mg) by mouth once daily for 5 days
Take 2 tablets (500mg) on day 1, then 1 tablet (250mg) daily on days 2-5

coding.display: C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

text.div: Presented with 3 days of worsening dyspnea, increased sputum volume, and change in sputum color to yellow-green, following an upper respiratory infection.
VS: BP 150/92, HR 110, RR 30, SpO2 85% RA (improved to 91% on 2L NC), Temp 37.6C. Diffuse expiratory wheeze and rhonchi, use of accessory muscles. CRP 45.3 mg/L (markedly elevated).
Acute exacerbation of COPD, likely infective (bacterial) trigger, moderate severity.

Nebulized bronchodilators, supplemental O2, oral prednisone 40mg daily x5 days, azithromycin 5-day course. Improved to SpO2 91% on room air trending up; discharged with close pulmonology follow-up in 3 days.

coding.display: olodaterol 0.0025 MG/ACTUAT / tiotropium 0.0025 MG/ACTUAT Inhalation Spray [Stiolto Respimat]

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth once daily

note.text: Stepped up from tiotropium monotherapy after AECOPD requiring ED visit; discontinue Spiriva HandiHaler.

resource.conclusion: Further decline in FEV1 (1.5L, GOLD stage 3 severe) following exacerbation. Recommend LABA/LAMA dual bronchodilator therapy and pulmonary rehabilitation.

text.div: Follow-up 3 days after AECOPD requiring ED visit. Sputum has returned to baseline color, but dyspnea remains worse than prior baseline.
VS: BP 136/86, HR 86, RR 22, SpO2 91%, Weight 71kg. FEV1 1.5L (GOLD 3, severe), FEV1/FVC 50%, PEF 180 L/min - decline from prior visit.
COPD with recent moderate exacerbation; pulmonary function has declined to GOLD stage 3 (severe) from stage 2.
Step up to LABA/LAMA combination (olodaterol/tiotropium, Stiolto Respimat), discontinue tiotropium monotherapy. Refer to pulmonary rehabilitation. Continue albuterol PRN. Reassess in 8-12 weeks.

coding.display: Eosinophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

resource.conclusion: FEV1 stable to slightly improved (1.6L vs 1.5L) since starting LABA/LAMA therapy, remains GOLD stage 3 (severe). No further exacerbations since step-up.

text.div: Patient reports improved exercise tolerance since starting Stiolto Respimat and beginning pulmonary rehabilitation. No exacerbations or ED visits since March. Occasional rescue inhaler use.
VS: BP 134/82, HR 84, RR 20, SpO2 93%, Weight 71.5kg. FEV1 1.6L, FEV1/FVC 52%, PEF 195 L/min - stable to slightly improved. Eosinophils 160/uL (normal).
COPD GOLD stage 3 (severe), stable on LABA/LAMA therapy, no exacerbations in 4 months since step-up and pulmonary rehab initiation.
Continue Stiolto Respimat and albuterol PRN. Continue pulmonary rehabilitation. Annual influenza vaccine due this fall. Follow-up in 6 months or sooner if symptoms worsen.
